best.title: The Day Cole Saved the Show
best.body:
At the school cafeteria, Cole set everything up. The llama practiced its part. The noisy spoon sat front and center. The whole show kind of depended on the noisy spoon working.
The noisy spoon just... gave up. It sat there. Completely uncooperative. The llama poked it. Nothing. The llama poked it again. Still nothing. Cole had eight seconds to fix this.
Cole took a breath, wobbled forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "snorble-doo!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. some pancakes sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day. A talking sandwich watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.
And from that day on, "snorble-doo" was the special word of the show. Everyone said it. Everyone meant it. Nobody knew what it meant. The llama approved. A faint pitch black glow hung over the scene by then. Later someone would swear they heard "foot soup" in the rafters. Cole kept feeling worried about the whole thing.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the llama. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

best.title: The Day Cole Saved the Show
best.body:
There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the luminous swamp. The co-star was the retired dragon. The crucial prop was a whistle.
Three seconds in, the retired dragon forgot the next part. Just blanked. Then the whistle fell off the table. Cole was on their own.
So Cole improvised. "tiny piano!" Cole yelled. Then waltzed accidentally. Then yelled "tiny piano!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT now. Everything in the room had picked up a faint impossible green tint. Cole kept feeling extremely unconvinced about the whole thing.
The retired wizard heckled. Genuinely heckled. The retired dragon hissed back. Cole kept performing. Conflict made the bit better.
It was a huge hit. A real one. Cole and the retired dragon got a huge clap, which was pretty cool because pillows do not even have hands. The new catchphrase "crusty pretzel" was now official. Somebody had brought thunder pancakes. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the retired dragon. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

best.title: The Hallway Pass Broke But Cole Did Not
best.body:
There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the museum basement. The co-star was the glittering octopus. The crucial prop was a hallway pass.
The hallway pass made a noise it should not have been able to make. Then it tipped sideways. Then it kept tipping. It was still tipping. The audience leaned in. Cole thought fast.
So Cole improvised. "sproing!" Cole yelled. Then scuttled with purpose. Then yelled "sproing!" again, louder. The pillows were INTO IT now. Cole kept feeling deeply snack-motivated about the whole thing. Somebody had brought extremely bold lasagna. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
The sparkly squid heckled. Genuinely heckled. The glittering octopus hissed back. Cole kept performing. Conflict made the bit better.
The pillows went wild. The glittering octopus took a bow. Cole took a bigger bow. "mystery jelly!" yelled Cole one final time. The room repeated it back. A faint eerie periwinkle glow hung over the scene by then.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the glittering octopus. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

best.title: Cole vs the Ninja
best.body:
At the pizza shop, Cole told the crow the plan. The plan was simple: invent a brand new dance. The crow nodded immediately. It was on.
There was just one problem: a ninja stood between Cole and the goal. "Nope," said the ninja. "Not today. Not invent a brand new dance. Not on my watch."
Cole suddenly pulled out a sunset orange flag and waved it dramatically. The ninja hesitated. The ninja had not been briefed on the flag. The flag took the ninja out of the moment for exactly long enough. Somewhere in there, Cole tiptoed for emphasis. Throughout, Cole stayed silly. Steadily silly. Somebody had brought popcorn. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
And then Cole did it. Cole invented a brand new dance, right in front of the ninja. "fluffypants!" yelled Cole. The crow took a small bow on Cole's behalf. Later someone would swear they heard "plop" in the rafters.
Back home, Cole replayed it in their head: how the crow had been right there, how it had all worked out. The crow curled up. Tomorrow could be just as good.

best.title: Cole and the Capybara Cross the Wobbly Bridge
best.body:
At the bus stop, Cole had decided. Today: cross the wobbly bridge. The capybara was on board. Quietly. Without making it a thing.
The algorithm ghost stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt socially exhausted about it. The algorithm ghost did not look like it was going to move on its own.
Cole speed-ran in a way that read as professionally unhinged. The algorithm ghost had not budgeted for this. Cole did not break eye contact.
The algorithm ghost tried to introduce some sour candy into the negotiation. It was a stretch. Cole did not negotiate. Cole kept moving. Everything in the room had picked up a faint midnight blue tint. Later someone would swear they heard "gloop" in the rafters.
Cole cross the wobbly bridge. The algorithm ghost said nothing. Win logged. No comment from the capybara, because the capybara was already moving on. Once, very quietly, Cole muttered "boing" under their breath.
In bed that night, Cole thought about how it had gone. The capybara was a co-conspirator now. Quietly proud. Not posting about it. Some wins are just for you.

worst.title: How Cole Found the Lost Mitten
worst.body:
Cole packed a tiny bag and called the lamb. The lamb came running. Cole led the way.
"Lost mitten!" yelled Cole. The lamb turned its head. "Lost mitten!" yelled Cole again, louder. The lamb yelled it back.
Cole built a tiny fort around the lost mitten. The lamb approved. "Safe now," said Cole. Cole bounced one more time, just to make a point. Everything in the room had picked up a faint lavender tint.
Cole pulled the lamb into a small pillow fort. "We made it," said Cole. The lamb yawned.

worst.title: Cole and the Corn
worst.body:
Cole spotted the chick from across the room. Cole waved. The chick waved back.
Cole pointed at the corn. "Corn!" said Cole. "Corn!" said the chick.
Cole put the corn on top of the chick's head. The chick held still. Cole clapped. Cole peeked one more time, just to make a point. Everything in the room had picked up a faint red tint.
Now Cole is sleepy. The chick is sleepy too. Good night, chick. Good night, Cole.

worst.title: Cole and the Koala
worst.body:
Cole opened a door and the koala tumbled in. "Hi!" said Cole. "Hi back!" said the koala — somehow.
Cole held up the foam finger and did a little dance. The koala copied the dance. Then they made up a new one together.
Cole carried the foam finger carefully across the room. The koala followed, watching every step. "Steady," said Cole. Cole bounced one more time, just to make a point. Everything in the room had picked up a faint leafy green tint.
Cole tucked the koala in first, then climbed in too. "Same time tomorrow?" said Cole. The koala was already half asleep.

worst.title: The Leaf and the Bear
worst.body:
"Come on, bear!" said Cole. The bear came. Cole led the way.
Cole pointed at the leaf. "Leaf!" said Cole. "Leaf!" said the bear.
Cole picked up the leaf. Cole held it up high. "Look!" said Cole. The bear looked. Cole crawled one more time, just to make a point. A faint red glow hung over the scene by then.
Now Cole is sleepy. The bear is sleepy too. Good night, bear. Good night, Cole.

worst.title: Cole and the Dramatic Cape
worst.body:
Cole packed a tiny bag and called the bunny. The bunny came running. Cole led the way.
Cole built a tiny fort around the dramatic cape. The bunny approved. "Safe now," said Cole.
Cole held up the dramatic cape and did a little dance. The bunny copied the dance. Then they made up a new one together. Cole flopped one more time, just to make a point. A faint leafy green glow hung over the scene by then.
Cole tucked the bunny in first, then climbed in too. "Same time tomorrow?" said Cole. The bunny was already half asleep.
